cancer_history_summary:
Age: 64
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma
Histology: Adenocarcinoma
Current extent: Metastatic disease with hepatic metastases (dominant Segment VII lesion 4.6 cm, several smaller lesions), peritoneal carcinomatosis with multiple nodules up to 2.3 cm, moderate-volume malignant ascites, minimally enlarged retroperitoneal lymph nodes, no distant solid-organ progression elsewhere, ECOG 1
Biomarkers: HER2 IHC 3+ / ISH amplification; MET gene amplification; FGFR2 amplification; KRAS p.G12D mutation; PIK3CA p.H1047R mutation; PD‑L1 Combined Positive Score 15%; CEA 18 ng/mL (rising); Albumin 3.2 g/dL (hypoalbuminemia); LDH borderline high 280 U/L
Treatment history:
# First‑line (dates unavailable): CAPOX (capecitabine oxaliplatin) combined with trastuzumab – resulted in disease stabilization then progression prompting regimen change.
# Subsequent line (post‑CAPOX failure): FOLFIRI (folinic acid, fluorouracil, irinotecan) – discontinued after radiological progression.
# Local therapy: Radiation to lumbar vertebral body L3 (osteoblastic metastasis) achieving local control; no new bony sites thereafter.
# Maintenance (concomitant with subsequent systemic therapy): Oral capecitabine (Xeloda) 1000 mg/m² twice daily Days 1–14 q21d – intermittent adherence due to nausea.
# Third‑line (ongoing as of 6 Jun 2025): Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg IV every 3 weeks – began after failing chemotherapy; initial stable disease, now progressive disease evidenced by expanding peritoneal implants, larger hepatic lesion, new ascites; last dose administered 6 days prior to 25 June 2025.
Supportive/treatment-related adverse effects recorded: Grade 2 hypothyroidism managed with levothyroxine 75 μg daily; Grade 3 anemia requiring packed red blood cell transfusion and initiation of ferric supplement and epoetin alfa; mild fatigue; peripheral edema related to ascites.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of common exclusion criteria – no untreated central nervous system metastases, cardiac insufficiency, significant respiratory compromise, uncontrolled hypertension, active autoimmune disease prohibiting immunotherapy, serious infectious comorbidity, or impaired hepatic/renal function exceeding eligibility thresholds.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 55
Sex: Female
Cancer type: Ovary (low‑grade serous ovarian carcinoma)
Histology: Low‑grade serous carcinoma
Current extent: Persistent peritoneal implants (≤1.2 cm) and a solitary para‑aortic lymph node (~1.9 cm); disease stable on imaging and CA‑125 trending plateaus (“stable disease”) while on endocrine therapy
Biomarkers: KRAS G12D pathogenic variant (allelic fraction ≈45%); NRAS Q61K low‑frequency subclonal variant; PIK3CA E545K low‑frequency variant; CCNE1 amplification (~3‑fold copy number gain); TPTP53 wild‑type; no deleterious BRCA1/BRCA2 mutations; hormonal milieu consistent with post‑menopausal estradiol <20 pg/mL (consistent with aromatase inhibition); PD‑L1 not reported
Treatment history:
# 02/2023: Total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo‑ovariectomy, comprehensive surgical staging (curative intent)
# 11/2023‑02/2024: Adjuvant carboplatin (AUC 5) + paclitaxel (175 mg/m²) IV, 4 cycles; best response transient disease stabilization, thereafter defined as platinum‑refractory
# 03/2025‑present: Letrozole 2.5 mg orally daily continuous; disease stabilized per RECIST and CA‑125 plateau; tolerated with Grade 1 arthralgia managed with acetaminophen/NSAIDs; supplemental calcium + Vitamin D instituted
# Planned/not yet commenced: Weekly paclitaxel monotherapy (considered) and enrollment discussions for KRAS‑directed or MEK/CDK pathway trials (not implemented)
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of common boilerplate exclusion criteria (ECOG 0‑1, no untreated central nervous system disease, adequate hepatic and renal function, no active serious infections, cardiovascular status uncompromised, no interstitial lung disease, HIV/hepatitis negative as far as recorded).

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 68  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Cutaneous malignant melanoma  
Histology: Malignant melanoma (skin origin)  
Current extent: Metastatic – bilateral pulmonary nodules representing lung-only metastases; no detectable extrapulmonary disease as of latest imaging (June 2025)  
Biomarkers: BRAF V600E mutant; additional somatic alterations – TERT‑promoter mutation, CDKN2A loss, TP53 R175H missense, NF1 truncation; PD‑L1 testing not recorded; LDH mildly elevated historically (peak 285 U/L) but within reference limit on most recent draw (215 U/L)  
  
Treatment history:  
# 12/2023: Wide local excision of left forearm melanoma (2 cm margins) and sentinel lymph node biopsy (positivity in 2/3 nodes with extranodal capsular extension) – curative intent surgery.  
# 01/2024‑05/2025: Adjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg IV q3 weeks × 16 cycles (total 12 months); completed without serious adverse event besides Grade 2 dermatitis which responded to topical steroids. Disease-free on serial imaging until June 2025.  
# 06/22/2025‑~07/2025: First three cycles of combined checkpoint inhibition – ipilimumab 3 mg/kg IV + nivolumab 1 mg/kg IV every 21 days (intention for four induction doses). Achieved partial radiographic response of pulmonary metastases (average ↓30 %). Experienced Grade 1 pruritus and xerostomia; after third ipilimumab infusion developed Grade 3 immune‑mediated colitis requiring hospitalization, high‑dose IV methylprednisolone followed by prolonged oral prednisone taper.  
# 08/2025‑present: Continuation of nivolumab monotherapy 240 mg IV every 2 weeks (maintenance) after permanent cessation of ipilimumab owing to colitis. Persistent partial response on repeat CT; mild transaminitis (AST/ALT ≈ ≤2× upper limit) monitored closely. Prednisone tapers completing soon; patient remains off steroids or on very low dose.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No uncontrolled brain metastases, no active infectious diseases (HBV/HCV negative, HIV non‑reactive), cardiovascular function stable (hypertension controlled), renal function normal, hepatic enzymes minimally elevated but <3× ULN, no congestive heart failure, no pneumonitis, no current grade > 2 immune‑related adverse events, performance status ECOG 0‑1.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 19  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Osteogenic sarcoma (conventional high‑grade osteosarcoma) of the distal right femur  
Histology: High‑grade conventional osteosarcoma, ≥85 % necrosis, negative surgical margins  
Current extent: Metastatic – solitary progressing pulmonary metastasis in the right lower lobe (now 2.0 × 1.8 cm)  
Biomarkers: None reported (comprehensive sarcoma NGS pending)  
Treatment history:  
# Mid‑2024: Neoadjuvant MAP (high‑dose methotrexate, doxorubicin, cisplatin) – completed prior to surgery  
# Late‑2024 (approximately Aug 2024): Wide limb‑preserving resection of distal right femur with endoprosthetic reconstruction; pathology confirmed high‑grade conventional osteosarcoma, ≥85 % necrosis, clear margins  
# Early‑2025 (through Apr 2025): Adjuvant MAP – six cycles completed uneventfully, no major toxicities, maintained normal blood counts and organ function  
# 01‑Jun‑2025 onward: Started salvage combination chemotherapy for presumed metastatic disease – ifosfamide 2 g/m² IV day 1‑5 + etoposide 100 mg/m² IV day 1‑5, planned every 21 days; received at least two cycles as of 25-Jun‑2025  
Best response to salvage therapy: Progressive disease evidenced by increase of pulmonary nodule from 1.2 cm (28‑Jun‑2025 scan) to 2.0 cm (25‑Jun‑2025 scan)  
Planned interventions: Image‑guided core needle biopsy of pulmonary nodule; multidisciplinary evaluation for either video‑assist thoracoscopic wedge resection or stereotactic body radiotherapy; continuation of ifosfamide/etoposide pending pathology and tolerance; supportive measures (mesna, uro‑alkalinisation, pegfilgrastim) outlined
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of typical trial exclusion factors – no untreated/uncontrolled brain metastases, acceptable cardiopulmonary reserve, normal renal/hepatic laboratory parameters, ECOG performance status 0, no active infections (HIV/Hepatitis), no autoimmune pneumonitis, and no concurrent malignancies.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 34
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Appendiceal well‑differentiated neuroendocrine tumour (GEPNET)
Histology: Well‑differentiated NET, Grade 2 (Ki‑67 approx 8%)
Current extent: Metastatic to liver (two known lesions, segments III and VI); disease stable per RECIST as of June 2025; no extraperitoneal spread detected
Biomarkers: KRAS G12D mutation; BRAF V600E mutation; CDKN2A loss; MYC amplification; Strong somatostatin receptor‑2 expression on ^68Ga‑DOTATATE PET (SUVmax ~12); Serum chromogranin A persistently modestly elevated (~110 ng/mL); urinary 5‑HIAA within normal limits; no actionable EGFR, RET, or HER2 abnormalities reported
Treatment history:
# Age 24: Appendectomy for incidentally found appendiceal NET, pathological stage pT1 N0 M0, clear margins
# Age 25‑27: Temozolomide + bevacizumab intravenously; attained partial response; transitioned to octreotide LAR 30 mg intramuscular every 28 days beginning circa age 26 owing to confirmed SSTR2 positivity
# Age 29‑31: Received four cycles of Lutetium‑177 (^177Lu)-DOTATATE peptide receptor radionuclide therapy; resultant marked shrinkage of hepatic lesion in segment III (<0.6 cm) and complete radiographic disappearance of prior peritoneal implants
# Age 33 (June 2025): Development of new 1.0‑cm hypervascular hepatic metastasis in segment VI indicating progressive disease; instituted everolimus 10 mg orally once daily whilst continuing octreotide LAR 30 mg IM q28 days; best observed response: stable disease
# Age 34 (June 2025): Continued combined everolimus + octreotide regimen; serial MRI confirms unchanged sizes of hepatic lesions (segments III and VI); maintained performance status ECOG 0‑1; adjunct medications include lisanopril 10 mg daily for hypertension and atorvastatin 20 mg nightly for everolimus‑associated hyperlipidemia
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of common trial exclusion criteria (uncontrolled hypertension resolved, lipids medically managed, adequate hepatic and renal function, no cardiac insufficiency, no interstitial lung disease, no active infections, ECOG ≤ 1).

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 66
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Right breast invasive lobular carcinoma
Histology: Infiltrative lobular carcinoma, estrogen-receptor positive (≈80%), progesterone-receptor negative, HER2‑low, Ki‑67 ∼30%
Current extent: Metastatic disease with a single hepatic lesion in segment IV (now 1.5 x 1.4 cm, slightly decreased) and osteoblastic bone metastases limited to the thoracic spine; overall disease classified as stable/metabolic partial response
Biomarkers: 
- ER+: positive
- PR−: negative
- HER2‑low (IHC 1+ / FISH non-amplified)
- Ki‑67 ≈30%
- Genetic alterations: CDH1 mutation, PIK3CA E545K missense, TP53 mutation, ESR1 D538G ligand-binding domain substitution, FGFR2 alteration(s), CCND1 amplification, CDK12 loss; PALB2 frameshift variant (VUS) awaiting further interpretation
- Serum CEA 3.1 ng/mL (stable)
Treatment history:
# Approximately 2023 (age 63‑64): Surgical resection of primary right‑breast tumour (details not recorded) followed by adjuvant hormonal therapy initiation
# 2024 (approximately age 65): Six cycles of weekly paclitaxel (dose TBD); best response not explicitly stated
# Post‑paclitaxel 2024 onward: Transition to letrozole monotherapy; during letrozole course a solitary liver metastasis emerged
# Early 2024 (aged 64): Commenced capecitabine 1250 mg/m² orally twice daily Days 1‑14 every 21 days; dose held for Grade 2 hand‑foot dermatitis, persistent diarrhea, or ANC <1500/μL; tolerability generally good after resolution of Grade 2 diarrhea; current regimen ongoing as of 2025‑06‑25, providing stable disease
# 2025‑06‑25: Supportive measures added – intravenous zoledronic acid 4 mg q12 wks for bone metastases, calcium/Vit D supplementation, topical urea cream for HFS prevention, anti‑diarrheal rescue kit maintained
# Future considerations discussed: Alpelisib + fulvestrant targeting PIK3CA mutation upon progression; enrollment possibilities in HER2‑low ADC or novel SERD trials
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of common boilerplate exclusion criteria – ECOG 1, adequate marrow, renal (Creatinine 0.92 mg/dL), hepatic (AST/ALT <40 U/L), cardiovascular stability, no untreated/uncontrolled central nervous system disease, no active serious infections, manageable peripheral neuropathy (≤ Grade 1), no prohibitive dermatologic adverse effects.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 62
Sex: Female
Cancer type: Glioblastoma, IDH‑wildtype, WHO Grade IV
Histology: High‑grade astrocytic tumour consistent with glioblastoma
Current extent: Progressive disease with new non‑enhancing infiltrative right posterior parietal lesion (~2 cm) evident on MRI 2025‑06‑20; no extracranial metastasis
Biomarkers: IDH‑wt; EGFR amplification; PTEN pathway alterations; MGMT promoter unmethylated; other routine panels negative/not reported
Treatment history:
# Approx. 2023 (age 60): Maximally safe gross total resection of left temporoparietooccipital tumour; pathology confirms IDH‑wt glioblastoma
# 2023: Concurrent external beam radiotherapy 60 Gy with concomitant temozolomide (Stupp protocol)
# Late 2023–early 2024 (age 61): Adjuvant temozolomide cycles completed; transitioned to observational follow‑up
# Early 2025 (age 63): Local progression treated with bevacizumab 10 mg/kg IV q2 weeks; interim improvement then held temporarily owing to grade 3 proteinuria; restarted once proteinuria improved, providing disease stability through ages 64–66
# Mid‑2025 (age ~66): Re‑operation demonstrated recurrent GBM with EGFR amplification, PTEN alterations, unmethylated MGMT
# Ages 67–71 (ongoing until Jun 2025): Compassionate‑use Phase I trial – investigational PDGFRα tyrosine kinase inhibitor combined with low‑dose temozolomide; tolerability notable for grade 2 maculopapular rash (resolved); serial MRIs showing stable disease up to age 68
# 2025‑06‑25: Decision made to discontinue experimental PDGFRα inhibitor/temozolomide combo due to radiographic progression; initiate bevacizumab monotherapy 10 mg/kg IV every 14 days (first infusion planned shortly after visit)
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: Presence of hypertension with persistent systolic pressures ≥150 mmHg (requiring escalation of antihypertensive therapy) and trace proteinuria attributed to prior bevacizumab exposure; neither condition meets outright exclusion but warrant careful monitoring during anti‑angiogenic therapy. No overt cardiac insufficiency, serious hepatic or renal dysfunction, infectious comorbidity, or untreated central nervous system complications identified.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 45
Sex: Female
Cancer type: Breast cancer
Histology: Invasive ductal carcinoma, estrogen receptor–positive, HER2‑negative
Current extent: Metastatic – hepatic involvement with three liver lesions (Segment IV 2.5 cm, Segment VI 1.9 cm, Segment VIII 1.6 cm) showing interval growth consistent with disease progression; no extra‑hepatic sites visualized on abdominal MRI
Biomarkers: ER‑positive, PR status not specified, HER2‑negative
Treatment history:
# Ongoing systemic therapy (type not detailed in record) – continued through 2025‑06‑25; radiologic assessment indicates progression of hepatic metastases during this regimen
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of common boilerplate exclusion criteria

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 68
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Small‑cell lung cancer
Histology: Classical small‑cell carcinoma
Current extent: Metastatic (Stage IV) with progressive disease evidenced by new hepatic metastasis; remaining extracranial sites stable; ECOG 1‑2
Biomarkers: No molecular/genomic alterations documented; immunohistochemistry results not recorded
Treatment history:
# 2020 (age 63): Carboplatin AUC 5 + Etoposide 100 mg/m² Day 1‑3 Q21 d × 4 cycles; concurrent palliative external‑beam RT to L3 (8 Gy single fraction); best response partial response (~30 % tumour reduction); Grade 3 neutropenia managed with filgrastim
# 2021 (age 64): Completion of above chemotherapy; Prophylactic Cranial Irradiation 20 Gy in 5 fractions; continued observation; best response sustained disease control; ECOG 1
# 2022 (age 65): Amrubicin 35 mg/m² Day 1‑3 Q21 d × 2 cycles for biochemical rise and back pain; developed Grade 3 thrombocytopenia (requiring transfusion) and Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy; best response stable disease
# 2023‑2025 (age 66‑68): Lurbinectedin 3.2 mg/m² Q21 d initiated upon progression (adrenal and femur lesions); completed 5 cycles as of June 2025; modest decrease adrenal size, bone lesion stable; adverse effects included Grade 2 anemia (treated with oral ferrous sulphate) and subsequent Grade 3 neutropenia prompting dose reduction; best response stable disease/regional improvement
# Planned/future (post‑June 2025): Discussion of temozolomide monotherapy (fourth‑line) versus enrollment in phase I/II trial targeting DNA‑damage repair pathways; definitive choice pending shared decision making
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of commonly cited trial exclusion criteria (uncontrolled central nervous system disease, active serious infection, uncontrolled cardiac/hepatic/renaland pancreatic impairment, autoimmune pneumonitis, HIV/Hepatitis B/C infection, or poor performance status).

No evidence of commonly excluded conditions: no uncontrolled hypertension (BP average ≈137/83 mmHg on medication), no cardiovascular instability, normal hematologic indices, adequate hepatic and renal reserve, no active infections, no central nervous system involvement, performance status not specified but functional exam reassuring, and no contraindicating concomitant illnesses.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 76
Sex: Female
Cancer type: High‑grade serous ovarian carcinoma
Histology: High‑grade serous (ovarian) carcinoma
Current extent: Metastatic disease with stable hepatic metastases and new subcentimetric peritoneal implants suggesting radiographic progression
Biomarkers: CA‑125 48 U/mL (slight rise versus prior 44 U/mL); no specific germline/somatic alterations documented
Treatment history:
# Mid‑2024: Primary cytoreductive debulking surgery
# Late‑2024: Adjuvant carboplatin + paclitaxel, 6 cycles
# Early‑2025: Initiated niraparib maintenance; dose reduced owing to Grade 3 thrombocytopenia
# Early‑2025: Switched to second‑line trabectedin after progression on niraparib (exact duration undocumented)
# Spring 2025 (≈Mar 2025): Commenced rucaparib 600 mg PO twice daily after trabectedin progression; ongoing as of 2025‑06‑20, tolerated with managed anemia/Fatigue
# Approx. Apr 2025: Percutaneous radiofrequency ablation of dominant hepatic metastasis (initial size 4.2 cm)
# Approx. Apr 2025: Stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) to solitary para‑aortic lymph node, 30 Gy in 5 fractions
# 05/2025 onward: Continued rucaparib monotherapy; periodic monitoring with CA‑125, CBC/CMP, imaging
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of common exclusion criteria (performance status ECOG 1, adequate hematologic/hepatic/kidney function, no uncontrolled brain/metastases, no serious cardiopulmonary disease, no active infections).
